response.reasoning: Bogdan has 17+ years of professional software development, far exceeding the 6-year threshold. His current role at re:cinq explicitly involves TypeScript, Node.js, AI-agent infrastructure, and MCP framework development — mapping directly onto the job's primary stack and the AI agent requirement. Prior roles at Optilyz (TypeScript, AWS CDK, React, MongoDB) and RIDE Capital (Node.js, TypeScript, GraphQL) reinforce sustained depth in the core stack. Testing culture is deeply embedded: he built open-source testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), ran multi-day TDD workshops, and applied TDD/CI/CD practices in production at multiple employers. Java appears in his skills and was used at Altom (robotic arms, mobile orchestration tooling), partially satisfying the Kotlin/Java secondary requirement. Cloud and containerization are covered: AWS CDK at Optilyz, Kubernetes in GISCollective. The critical gap is Nest.js — the designated primary backend framework — which does not appear anywhere in the resume. Kotlin is also absent. Vitest is not explicitly listed, though his overall testing depth suggests fast ramp-up. The MIT CS degree satisfies the education requirement. On balance this is a strong profile with one significant named-framework gap.
strengths: Current role directly involves TypeScript, Node.js, AI-agent infrastructure, and MCP framework work — precise match to primary stack and AI agent requirement
Deep, demonstrated TDD and testing culture across roles, workshops, and open-source testing libraries built from scratch
Broad full-stack seniority across cloud (AWS CDK), containers (Docker, Kubernetes), multiple databases (MongoDB, MySQL, SQL), and frontend (React)
concerns: Nest.js — the primary backend framework for this role — is completely absent from the resume with no equivalent structured framework mentioned
Kotlin is not listed or referenced anywhere; Java exposure appears limited to embedded/robotics contexts rather than backend service development
Vitest and Playwright are not explicitly named, and relational DB depth (PostgreSQL) is not clearly evidenced despite SQL being listed as a skill
response.justification: Bogdan is a highly experienced full-stack engineer whose current role maps closely to the core of this position — TypeScript, Node.js, React, AI agent infrastructure, and developer tooling — and whose career demonstrates strong testing discipline and multi-language adaptability. The main risk is the complete absence of Nest.js, which is the designated primary backend framework; however, given his Node.js depth and long track record of picking up new frameworks across roles, the ramp-up risk is manageable rather than disqualifying. An interview should probe Nest.js familiarity, relational DB experience, and Kotlin exposure to confirm fit.
